name: Spicy Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon red chili powder (or 1/3 of a mild chili pepper, ground)
3 whole boned and skinned chicken breasts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a spice mill, small food processor, or mortar and pestle, grind garlic with the tamarind paste and gingerroot.
2. Add remaining spices; blend until smooth.
3. Gradually add water, one tablespoon at a time, blending after each addition until the mixture is a smooth paste.
4. Butterfly each chicken breast so that it yields a single, thin piece of meat by cutting horizontally through the thickest part of the meat and spreading it open.
5. Brush both sides of each breast with the spice paste and refrigerate for 2 to 4 hours (marinating for 1 hour at room temperature is possible but will result in less intense flavor).
6. Heat a charcoal grill until very hot, or set a cast-iron skillet over the highest heat on a stove.
7. Sear chicken for 3 minutes on each side or a bit longer until it is no longer pink (the cooking time should be minimal as the chicken is very thin).
8. Place the chicken on a serving platter and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
9. Serve with any combination of tortillas, rice, refried beans, guacamole, and chopped tomatoes.
Tamarind paste is available in Oriental markets or the oriental section of large supermarkets.
